summary: Implemented the main sword-afterimage rendering routine in src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c, taking ftCo_800C2600 from a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub to an 88.71% unmatched-item improvement and finishing ftCo_800C2FD8 from 98.99% to 100.00% per the decomp-dev bot. The PR also exposed the early sword-afterimage parameter fields in itSword_UnkBytes and Marth/Roy SwordAttrs as f32 scale values plus color/alpha bytes, and replaced an OSReport/__assert pattern with HSD_ASSERTREPORT for matching.

key_files.role: Implemented ftCo_800C2600 afterimage rendering and adjusted ftCo_800C2FD8 sword-item assertion/control flow.
Changed itSword_UnkBytes offsets +0/+4 from byte fields to f32 x0/x4, matching afterimage scale parameters.
Expanded SwordAttrs header fields at offsets 0x0-0x10 so Marth/Roy attributes can share the sword-afterimage layout.

what_changed: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in ftCo_800C2600 with a GX rendering path for sword afterimages: GX state setup, ring-buffer traversal, vertex generation, triangle-strip submission, and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
Added local AfterimageVtx with position plus RGBA fields and defined AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP as 0.0872664600610733f.
ftCo_800C2600 now early-returns unless arg1 == 2 and the fighter has more than one afterimage entry via (s8)(u8)fp->x2100.

The afterimage parameter source is selected from either a held It_Kind_Sword item via it_80285300, Link/Young Link dat_attrs at x64, or Marth/Roy dat_attrs at x78; many non-sword fighter kinds fall through without a parameter assignment in the diff evidence.
Generated inner/outer afterimage vertices from fp->x20B0 ring entries, fp->x20F8/x20FC scale values, cumulative distance, alpha interpolation from params->x8/x9, and colors from params->xA-x10.

Inserted curved subdivisions between afterimage entries using lbVector_CrossprodNormalized, lbVector_Angle, and lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis when the angle exceeds the AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP-derived subdivision threshold.
Changed ftCo_800C2FD8's held-item check from if/else plus OSReport/__assert to a switch on itGetKind with HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x16D, 0, "no afterimage item!\n") in the default case.
Changed itSword_UnkBytes so offsets +0 and +4 are f32 x0 and f32 x4 instead of eight separate u8 fields.
Expanded ftMars SwordAttrs from pad_x0[0x14] to f32 x0, f32 x4, u8 x8 through x10, then pad_x11[3], preserving later x14/x18/x1C fields.
smart_moves: Recognized that item swords, Link/Young Link sword attributes, and Marth/Roy sword attributes share the same early afterimage-parameter layout, allowing casts to itSword_UnkBytes* rather than duplicating layouts.
Used explicit offset-commented header fields while keeping placeholder names, improving type correctness for matching without overclaiming semantics for unknown fields.
Preserved match-sensitive signed byte behavior with casts like (s8)(u8)fp->x2100 around the afterimage-entry count.
Guarded distance interpolation with totalDist < 0.00001f -> 1.0f, avoiding a divide-by-near-zero path while matching the observed algorithm.
Reproduced the low-level GX state sequence and direct vertex submission instead of abstracting the renderer, which matters for decompilation codegen and side effects.
Kept the vertex-output loop shape, including four-at-a-time emission and a remainder label/goto, a likely codegen tactic for the original loop structure.
Switched the assertion path to HSD_ASSERTREPORT, which the merge commit and diff indicate was needed to finish ftCo_800C2FD8.
decomp_lessons: Header typing can be the key to a function match: changing opaque byte fields to f32 at offsets +0/+4 made the afterimage scale math express naturally in both item and fighter attribute layouts.
When the same binary data layout appears in item and fighter dat_attrs, define one shared-compatible struct but avoid semantic names until the fields are proven.
GX-heavy functions often need exact state-call order, direct GXPosition/GXColor emission, and final HSD_StateInvalidate calls; helper abstraction may harm matching.
Ring-buffer wrap logic can be match-sensitive. This PR preserves the three-entry wrap from index 0 back to 2 using x2101_bits_0to6 and nextIdx-style temporaries.
For near-complete functions, assertion macro choice and control-flow shape can be the last difference: ftCo_800C2FD8 reached 100% after the HSD_ASSERTREPORT/switch rewrite.
Distance-based and angle-based interpolation code may require explicit temporaries, nested scopes, and casts to reproduce original PowerPC register/stack lifetimes.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names ftCo_800C2600 and ftCo_800C2FD8; no semantic rename evidence was present.
Used uppercase macro style for AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP.
Introduced a small local-format typedef named AfterimageVtx with x/y/z and r/g/b/a fields.
Retained placeholder offset field names x0, x4, x8, x9, xA, etc. for itSword_UnkBytes and SwordAttrs rather than inventing semantic names.
Used offset comments in struct definitions, e.g. /* +0x0 */ f32 x0 and /*   +0 */ f32 x0, matching existing decomp header style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT instead of OSReport plus __assert when matching HSD-style assertions with fixed line numbers such as 0x7C and 0x16D.
Keep explicit casts such as (s8)(u8)fp->x2100 for signed-byte comparisons and loop bounds.
Use MSL/math_ppc sqrtf and existing lbVector helpers rather than hand-inlined vector math.
Preserve unusual triangle-strip setup details from the diff, including GXBegin(GX_TRIANGLESTRIP, GX_VTXFMT0, numVerts + 1) and initial vtx_buf[0] then vtx_buf[2] emission.
Maintain local stack buffers and sizes visible in the implementation, notably AfterimageVtx vtx_buf[152] and f32 cumDist[3].
Nested scopes and many single-purpose temporaries are useful for matching complex rendering code with interpolation and ring-buffer state.
A switch on itGetKind with a default assertion matched better than an if/else for ftCo_800C2FD8.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice; evidence is from the diff, merge metadata, and the decomp-dev bot report.
decomp-dev bot reported overall matched code 62.56% (+0.01%, +436 bytes).
decomp-dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/ft/ftafterimage ftCo_800C2FD8 from 98.99% to 100.00%.
decomp-dev bot reported ftCo_800C2600 improved from 0.22% to 88.71% (+2230 bytes) and .sdata2 from 0.00% to 87.50%.
Merge commit message notes: Match ftCo_800C2FD8, Match ftCo_800C2600, Remove target funcs, 100% of ftCo_800C2FD8, and Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish ftCo_800C2600 from 88.71% to 100%.
Search for semantic renames or further typing of itSword_UnkBytes fields x0, x4, x8-x10.
Check later Link, Young Link, Marth, and Roy attribute layout work around ftLk_DatAttrs x64 and MarsAttributes/SwordAttrs x78.
Search for other GX afterimage or trail rendering functions that reuse AfterimageVtx-style direct GX emission.
Find PRs replacing OSReport/__assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT for final matching bytes.
